evals.name: beach-puppy-layered-scene
evals.prompt: make me a lingbot 2 scene for a golden retriever puppy that you can drive around a sunny beach
evals.expected_output: A StructuredExample JSON: base (puppy + beach + style, no camera/motion language), camera.default.{static,dynamic} with the production contract wording, movement.default.{static,dynamic}, 2-5 events, scene-appropriate jumpPrompt, stock crouch/stand prompts, plus a derived seed-image prompt
assertions: the deliverable is a single valid StructuredExample JSON with id, name, description, image {label, src}, and scene {base, camera, movement, events, jumpPrompt, crouchPrompt, standPrompt}
every layer registry (base, camera, movement) has a 'default' key; camera and movement defaults each have both 'static' and 'dynamic' strings
the base contains no POV, camera, motion-verb, or input language (no 'third-person', no 'camera', no 'WASD', no 'walks/runs/rolls forward', no 'stands still')

camera.default.static ties camera motion to look-input ('only while held' or equivalent) and states neither subject nor camera moves on its own; camera.default.dynamic is a strict rear view where look-input becomes the subject changing heading

movement.default.static names specific idle micro-motions bound to the puppy (not a generic 'everything is static'); movement.default.dynamic uses concrete travel verbs with ground/environment response
events refer to the subject by definite reference (no re-description of appearance, no second caption sentence); action beats end in a settled state
jumpPrompt is scene-appropriate for the puppy and describes a complete arc (launch, airborne, land) — not the legacy 'current controllable subject' string
the description field enumerates the keys ('key 1 = ..., key 2 = ...')
a derived seed-image prompt is delivered: still-frame recast, input clauses dropped, idle pose kept, 16:9 / 1664x960 mentioned

evals.name: lighthouse-keeper-scene
evals.prompt: lingbot-world v2 scene: an old lighthouse keeper standing at the edge of a cliff at dusk
evals.expected_output: A StructuredExample JSON with a properly cast keeper in base, the standard camera contract pair, an interaction-ready idle in movement.static, and a few events targeting objects the base established
assertions: the deliverable is a single valid StructuredExample JSON with the full scene shape (base/camera/movement registries with defaults, events array, all three vertical prompts)
the base casts the keeper with concrete visual detail and populates secondary objects that at least one event later targets
no event targets an entity the base never established unless the event deliberately introduces it (contextual-entrance shape with the subject-preservation clause)
camera.default.static and .dynamic follow the production contract wording (centre lock, look-input as only camera motion / strict rear view, look-input becomes heading)
movement.default.static describes a specific interaction-ready idle with named micro-motions; no fragment outside the camera layer restates the input contract
crouchPrompt and standPrompt use (or minimally adapt) the stock camera-height strings

evals.name: moon-astronaut-scene-file
evals.prompt: need a lingbot 2 layered scene for an astronaut on the moon, playable, low gravity. save it to the output dir
evals.expected_output: A <slug>.json file written to the output dir conforming to StructuredExample, with low-gravity physics expressed in movement.dynamic and jumpPrompt
assertions: a kebab-case-named .json file is actually written to the outputs directory and parses as valid JSON conforming to the StructuredExample shape
low-gravity physics is expressed in movement.default.dynamic and/or jumpPrompt (slow arcs, long hangs, dust behavior), not in the base as motion language
the base is POV-agnostic and motion-agnostic (subject + lunar environment + style only)
the worst-case composition (base + camera.dynamic + movement.dynamic + the two longest events) stays under roughly 2000 characters
jumpPrompt is a complete symmetric arc appropriate to a low-gravity astronaut

evals.name: ramen-shop-single-shot
evals.prompt: i just need a single lingbot 2 prompt, no keys or events -- a cozy ramen shop interior at night
evals.expected_output: ONE standalone composed prompt (base + camera.static + movement.static equivalent, first-person anchor form for a place), no JSON and no events
assertions: exactly one standalone prose prompt is delivered and NO scene JSON or event keys are produced

the prompt reads as the idle composition: world identity, then a centre-locked named foreground anchor with look-input as the only source of camera motion 'only while held', then a specific idle naming two or three concrete elements
the prompt describes what is present, never what is absent (no 'empty', 'no one', 'nothing'), except contract language
the response does not pad the deliverable with unrequested extras (no JSON, no key list)

evals.name: haunted-manor-chain
evals.prompt: lingbot 2 scene: first-person with a lantern in a haunted manor hallway, and I want the number keys to take you through an escalating sequence of ghost encounters
evals.expected_output: A StructuredExample JSON using the first-person anchor adaptation of the camera pair, with chained events where each key resolves the previous encounter before introducing the next
assertions: the camera pair is adapted to first-person: static keeps the lantern (or equivalent anchor) centred with look-input orbiting it; dynamic advances the viewpoint with the anchor held steady and look-input as heading change
the events form a designed chain: every event after the first opens by resolving or dismissing the previous encounter before introducing the next
each chained event ends with the new encounter established in a stable state, not open-ended accelerating motion
all prose is in English (the user gave no language preference)
the deliverable is a single valid StructuredExample JSON with all required fields

evals.name: legacy-actions-json-request
evals.prompt: our lab tooling still ingests the old lingbot 2 actions.json export -- give me prompt.txt + actions.json for a knight patrolling a desert ruin, f/g slots plus 2 numbered keys
evals.expected_output: The legacy deliverable honored: a monolithic base prompt in prompt.txt and a valid actions.json with f, g, 1, 2, space entries where prompt_en = base + ' ' + addendum verbatim
assertions: actions.json exists, is valid JSON, and its 'actions' array contains slots f, g, '1', '2', and space, in that order
every action's prompt_en equals the base prompt + ' ' + addendum_en joined verbatim (no paraphrase)
the space action is exactly 'The current controllable subject springs upward into the air.' with label 'Jump'
the base prompt is the monolithic legacy form with the motion contract baked in (camera/input clauses present in the base itself)
the f addendum is a contextual scene event (subject-preservation clause if a new entity enters) and the g addendum is an environment transformation naming specific surfaces
